title: Make drain mode default and stop wasting no_work runs
description:
## Problem
Orca currently continues running after queue exhaustion when agents emit `result=no_work` with `loop_action=continue`, causing avoidable token burn.

## Decision intent
Drain mode should be the default operating behavior: once there is no actionable work, the loop should stop unless there is an explicit reason to retry.

## Scope
- Make drain semantics the default in loop policy.
- Define deterministic stop behavior for `no_work` that minimizes false stops from transient states/races.
- Preserve an explicit operator-controlled override for watch/poll mode when desired.
- Ensure behavior is deterministic in both bounded (`--runs N`) and unbounded runs.

## Acceptance criteria
- In default mode, sustained queue exhaustion leads to loop stop without repeated `no_work` token burn.
- Transient `no_work` cases (e.g. race/refresh window) can be retried in a controlled way.
- Behavior is documented in README and operator guide.

comments.author: agent-1
comments.text: Implemented drain-by-default no_work policy in harness: added ORCA_NO_WORK_DRAIN_MODE (drain|watch), ORCA_NO_WORK_RETRY_LIMIT (default 1), start/orca CLI flags (--drain/--watch/--no-work-retries), deterministic consecutive no_work stop handling in agent-loop.sh, and README/OPERATOR_GUIDE updates.

title: Improve status performance by avoiding full metrics rescans
description:
## Problem
`status.sh` repeatedly scans/parses full `metrics.jsonl`, which scales poorly and slows frequent polling.

## Scope
- Reduce status runtime cost by avoiding full-file parsing on every call (e.g. tail-window parsing and/or incremental summary cache).
- Keep output correctness for key counters and latest-run signals.
- Add a simple runtime benchmark/check target to prevent regressions.

## Acceptance criteria
- Typical status latency is materially reduced on larger metrics files.
- Key health/activity fields remain correct.
- Performance approach and limits are documented.

title: Align docs with local-main policy and runtime knob behavior
description:
## Problem
Core docs should reflect runtime behavior and local source-of-truth policy.

## Scope
- Update docs to codify local `main` as default source-of-truth for local Orca runs (including how divergence from `origin/main` is handled).
- Ensure runtime knob docs match implemented behavior for `ORCA_PRIMARY_REPO` and `ORCA_WITH_LOCK_PATH` injection.
- Cross-check for consistency across core operator docs (`README.md`, `OPERATOR_GUIDE.md`, and any directly related references).

## Acceptance criteria
- A new reader can infer local `main` default behavior unambiguously.
- Runtime knob documentation matches actual `start.sh`/`agent-loop.sh` behavior.
- No conflicting statements remain in the updated docs set.
close_reason: Docs now align with local-main policy and runtime knob behavior

comments.author: assistant
comments.text: Updated scope per operator direction: remove Option C/decision-log-based evaluation content from tasking; keep docs coherence focused on runtime behavior and local-main policy.

comments.author: agent-1
comments.text: Updated docs to codify local main as default local source-of-truth (with explicit divergence behavior vs origin/main), and aligned runtime knob docs for ORCA_PRIMARY_REPO/ORCA_WITH_LOCK_PATH defaults, validation, and injection behavior with start.sh + agent-loop.sh.

title: Add safe run-branch garbage collection command
description:
## Problem
Run branches (`swarm/*-run-*`) accumulate over time and create local ref clutter.

## Scope
- Add a safe garbage-collection command/script for stale run branches.
- Only prune branches that are merged/obsolete and not active in any worktree/session.
- Provide dry-run mode and clear reporting.

## Acceptance criteria
- Operators can safely clean historical run branches without impacting active work.
- Cleanup command has dry-run and explicit safety checks.
- Docs include when/how to run cleanup.

comments.author: agent-1
comments.text: Implemented gc-run-branches command with dry-run default and safety guards: only prune swarm/*-run-* when merged into main and not active in worktree/tmux session. Updated orca.sh dispatch + README/OPERATOR_GUIDE docs. Validation: bash -n orca.sh gc-run-branches.sh and dry-run invocation in this worktree.

comments.author: agent-1
comments.text: Completed and merged to main. Added ./orca.sh gc-run-branches command with dry-run default, merged-into-main filter, active worktree/session protection, and clear reporting; updated README and OPERATOR_GUIDE usage/docs.

title: Fix status loop action reporting from metrics summary
description:
## Problem
`status.sh` displays per-agent `loop=n/a` even when runs produced loop actions, because it reads `.loop_action` at the top level of metrics rows while loop data is stored under `.summary.loop_action`.

## Scope
- Update `status.sh` parsing for agent activity rows to read:
  - `.summary.loop_action`
  - `.summary.loop_action_reason`
- Keep backward compatibility if old rows used top-level fields (optional fallback).
- Validate output formatting still works when loop fields are missing.

## Acceptance criteria
- `orca status` shows actual loop action and reason for new metrics rows.
- Existing output remains readable when loop fields are absent.

comments.author: agent-1
comments.text: Implemented in status.sh: agent activity now reads loop_action/loop_action_reason from summary.* first with fallback to legacy top-level fields. Validated with synthetic metrics rows covering summary-only, legacy-only, and missing loop fields.

title: Add status run-in-progress indicator and session scoping
description:
## Problem
During long runs, status can appear stale until metrics are appended. Also old sessions can pollute current view.

## Scope
- Add a run-in-progress indicator per active agent/session.
- Add session-scoping/filter options for status views (e.g. current session prefix or explicit session id).
- Keep backward-compatible defaults for existing operator workflows.

## Acceptance criteria
- Operators can tell if an agent is actively running vs idle/stalled.
- Status can focus on relevant current sessions without historical noise.

title: Fix drain-stop loop-action observability ordering
description:
## Problem
Drain-stop observability is inconsistent: in `agent-loop.sh`, run observability artifacts are finalized before no-work drain-stop logic mutates loop action/reason, so metrics/summary can miss the actual stop reason.

## Scope
- Ensure final recorded run artifacts (metrics row + summary markdown) reflect post-decision loop action/reason for drain-stop exits.
- Keep deterministic ordering so stop reason is visible in both machine-readable and human-readable artifacts.
- Add a regression check covering a no-work drain stop path.

## Acceptance criteria
- For a run that triggers drain stop, metrics and summary both include `loop_action=stop` and the drain stop reason.
- No regression for normal completed/blocked/no_work (non-stop) runs.

comments.author: agent-1
comments.text: Implemented fix in agent-loop: apply no_work drain-stop loop_action/reason before finalize_run_observability so summary.md and metrics.jsonl capture final stop reason. Added regression script tests/regression_drain_stop_observability.sh and validated it passes.

title: Add lightweight protocol telemetry for Option C manual reversibility
description:
## Problem
Option C is intentionally autonomy-first and reversible by operator decision, but current telemetry is weak for manual reversal decisions (e.g., protocol drift/race trends).

## Scope
Add lightweight, non-enforcing telemetry to improve manual review quality:
- Capture explicit counters/events for:
  - claim publication race/failure events
  - queue helper failures
  - merge helper failures (including `.beads` rejection)
  - lock timeout/contention events
- Emit machine-readable data (metrics JSONL and/or dedicated events JSONL).
- Surface a concise rollup in `status.sh` for recent window.

## Constraints
- Do not add hard runtime enforcement.
- Keep implementation cheap and deterministic.

## Acceptance criteria
- Operator can answer “is protocol drift/race overhead increasing?” from artifacts without log spelunking.
- Status output includes a compact recent telemetry summary.

comments.author: assistant
comments.text: Operator decision: telemetry for Option C evaluation will be tracked manually outside tasking. Closing this issue as out-of-scope for implementation.

title: Clarify --runs as max upper bound with early-stop precedence
description:
## Problem
`--runs` semantics are ambiguous in docs/mental model. It should represent a hard upper bound, not an instruction to spend runs when there is no work.

## Scope
- Define `--runs` as max iterations (upper bound), always subject to earlier stop conditions (drain/no_work, explicit stop, failure policy).
- Update command help, README, and operator guide wording to remove ambiguity.
- Add regression checks for early termination before max run count.

## Acceptance criteria
- Running with `--runs N` never forces extra `no_work` iterations after stop conditions are met.
- Docs and help text consistently describe `--runs` as an upper bound.

title: Fix ORCA_BASE_REF propagation from start.sh to agent-loop.sh
description:
## Problem
`ORCA_BASE_REF` is honored by `setup-worktrees.sh`, but tmux-launched `agent-loop.sh` does not reliably receive it from `start.sh`. In testing, setting `ORCA_BASE_REF=does/not/exist` still resulted in run branches based on `main`.

## Scope
- Ensure `start.sh` injects `ORCA_BASE_REF` into each tmux session environment (same pattern as other runtime knobs).
- Add startup validation for `ORCA_BASE_REF` when set (clear error if ref is invalid/unresolvable).
- Keep direct `agent-loop.sh` behavior consistent with documented override semantics.
- Update docs where needed to describe override behavior accurately.

## Acceptance criteria
- With `ORCA_BASE_REF` set to a valid ref, run branches are created from that ref.
- With `ORCA_BASE_REF` set to an invalid ref, startup fails fast with clear error.
- Setup and run flows have consistent override behavior.
close_reason: Completed: ORCA_BASE_REF propagation + fail-fast validation implemented

comments.author: agent-1
comments.text: Implemented ORCA_BASE_REF propagation from start.sh into tmux-launched agent-loop sessions and added fail-fast ORCA_BASE_REF commit-resolution validation in start.sh, setup-worktrees.sh, and direct agent-loop startup. Validated invalid ref failures in all three scripts.

title: Deprecate discovery-log feature while keeping follow-up issue creation
description:
## Problem
Discovery-specific machinery appears unused and adds prompt/schema/runtime complexity (discovery log paths, dedicated discovery protocol section, mandatory discovery summary fields), while follow-up issue creation itself remains useful.

## Scope
- Remove/deprecate discovery-log specific plumbing:
  - prompt placeholders and env vars dedicated only to discovery log files
  - discovery log file creation/touching if no longer needed
  - heavy discovery protocol wording in prompt/docs
- Keep follow-up issue creation guidance in queue workflow.
- Simplify run summary contract by making discovery-specific fields optional or removing them (with a compatibility strategy for existing metrics consumers).
- Update docs and validation logic consistently.

## Acceptance criteria
- Agents are still guided to create follow-up issues when needed.
- Discovery-log-only paths and instructions are removed.
- Summary/metrics schema and status tooling remain coherent after simplification.

title: Add orca status quick/full modes for operator ergonomics
description:
## Problem
`orca status` is expensive and noisy for frequent monitoring; operators need a lightweight view by default.

## Scope
- Add status modes/flags, e.g. `--quick` and `--full`.
- Define a minimal quick view (health summary, active sessions, latest agent activity, high-signal alerts).
- Keep detailed sections available behind full mode.

## Acceptance criteria
- Quick mode is substantially faster and focused on active operations.
- Full mode retains current deep diagnostic output.
- CLI help documents both modes and defaults.

title: Prefer local main as default base ref in setup and run flows
description:
## Problem
Orca still has local-main precedence gaps: both worktree setup and per-run branch creation can prefer `origin/main`, which causes stale execution when local `main` is ahead.

## Desired behavior
For local Orca operation, local `main` is the default source of truth unless explicitly overridden.

## Scope
- Update base-ref selection in `setup-worktrees.sh` so default preference is:
  1. `ORCA_BASE_REF` (explicit override)
  2. local `main`
  3. `origin/main`
  4. current branch fallback
- Update run-branch base selection in `agent-loop.sh` to follow the same precedence.
- If both local `main` and `origin/main` exist and differ, emit an explicit warning with ahead/behind counts.
- Keep existing ability to force a ref through `ORCA_BASE_REF`.
- Update docs (`README.md`, `OPERATOR_GUIDE.md`) to match behavior.

## Acceptance criteria
- New worktrees default from local `main` when present.
- Per-run branches also default from local `main` when present.
- Divergence warning is visible when `main` and `origin/main` differ.
- Documentation matches implemented precedence across setup + run flows.

comments.author: agent-1
comments.text: Implemented base-ref precedence alignment in setup-worktrees.sh and agent-loop.sh: ORCA_BASE_REF -> main -> origin/main -> current branch; added divergence warnings with ahead/behind counts when main and origin/main differ; updated README.md and OPERATOR_GUIDE.md; validated with bash -n setup-worktrees.sh agent-loop.sh.

title: Propagate ORCA_PRIMARY_REPO and ORCA_WITH_LOCK_PATH from start.sh
description:
## Problem
Documentation says `ORCA_PRIMARY_REPO` and `ORCA_WITH_LOCK_PATH` are injected into agent runs, but `start.sh` does not pass them into tmux session env. Overrides are silently ignored.

## Scope
- Wire `ORCA_PRIMARY_REPO` and `ORCA_WITH_LOCK_PATH` through `start.sh` into per-session env, alongside existing helper vars.
- Add startup validation in `start.sh`:
  - `ORCA_PRIMARY_REPO` points to a valid git worktree.
  - `ORCA_WITH_LOCK_PATH` is executable.
- Ensure logs from `agent-loop.sh` reflect overridden values when set.
- Update docs only if behavior intentionally differs.

## Acceptance criteria
- Running `start.sh` with overrides results in agent-loop using overridden primary repo and lock helper.
- Invalid override values fail fast at startup with clear errors.
- README/operator docs are consistent with runtime behavior.

comments.author: agent-1
comments.text: Implemented in start.sh: pass ORCA_PRIMARY_REPO and ORCA_WITH_LOCK_PATH into tmux agent env, add startup validation for both overrides, and updated README startup checks. Validated with bash -n plus fail-fast checks for invalid repo path and non-executable lock helper.

comments.author: agent-1
comments.text: Implemented in start.sh: propagate ORCA_PRIMARY_REPO and ORCA_WITH_LOCK_PATH into tmux-launched agent-loop env, and validate both overrides at startup. README startup checks updated; verified with bash -n and invalid override fail-fast runs.

comments.author: agent-1
comments.text: Completed and merged to main (1513ca8). start.sh now validates ORCA_PRIMARY_REPO as git worktree and ORCA_WITH_LOCK_PATH as executable, and injects both vars into tmux-launched agent-loop sessions. README startup validation list updated accordingly.
